programmes.show_description: Arthur meets up with an old friend from the Army but then loses him and his trousers. S2 E6: The Beer Hunter
Alf's eyesight is worsening, which is fine until he accidentally enters the Ladies' toilet and is arrested as a sex pest. S1 E2
Robin has failed his cookery exam and considers moving back to Southampton, but the girls are far from pleased at the thought of his mate Larry moving in. S2 E6: Carry Me Back to Old Southampton
David is less than happy to find that the obnoxious Kev Wilson, a man who has always done better than him and let him know it since childhood, is staying at the hotel. S2 E6: Snap

We climb on board the Andalucian Explorer for a once-in-a-lifetime journey that takes in the exotic sights and sounds of southern Spain from a historic Pullman-style railway carriage.

Arthur gets involved with Clive Cosgrove, who has several businesses that are each run by a woman he is married to. S5 E3: A Number of Old Wives' Tales
Elizabeth and her daughter return to Eaton Place, but Sarah dislikes Nanny Webster as she is very set in her ways and seems not to be able to cope as she used to. S2 E8: Out of the Everywhere
Mrs Boswell has a bit of trouble with her brood. Billy returns to his estranged wife Julie, while Adrian reaches a plateau. S5 E5
Harold thinks the family home needs central heating; Albert is delighted until he hears that his son plans to install it all himself. S4 E3: Those Magnificent Men and Their Heating Machines
The staff discover that the station is a hundred years old and decide to have a street party to celebrate. S2 E10: Ton Up

When Mildred is going to the Floral Society annual dinner dance but George doesn't want to, Robin agrees to accompany Mildred to the dance and leaves George rather jealous. S6 E3: The Generation Game
Gary bumps into his former talent agent, Sidney Wix, which leads to an unwelcome collaboration with superstar ukulele player, George Formby. S3 E7: Turned Out Nice Again
When Jack visits Sir Manners - the father of his late officer - and gives him a present, Manners offers him a job; elsewhere, Jack saves Tom from a beating. S1 E4: Swords and Pick Handles

John Sullivan's bittersweet sitcom. Vince asks his father for advice about Penny while Penny hears about her mother's surprising past. S1 E4: Fatherly Advice

Classic sitcom with one-man disaster area Frank Spencer. Frank and Betty go for a spin in his new car and end up having a clifftop picnic. S2 E1: Cliffhanger

Rigsby decides to move on, give up on Miss Jones and file for divorce - he places an advert in the local newspaper, but his search for love doesn't run entirely smoothly. S4 E4: Pink Carnations
Terry is disappointed that Bob has asked someone else to be his best man - after a few drinks at the restaurant, they start to chat up a couple of pretty girls. S1 E10: The Old Magic
Arthur enters the food business, but when the chef he employs goes on the run from a group of gangsters, Arthur is left to cater for a policeman's retirement party. S8 E10: Too Many Crooks
Mateo's mother-in-law is threatening to kill him. Tiger and Terri come up with a plan to get Clive's 5,000 euros back from Monty. Riannon arrives - and there is no sign of Donald. S7 E7

Arthur has become a wine merchant and brought from dodgy Clive Stannard, but he is robbed of his money when a masseuse visits his hotel room and blames Arthur. S2 E7: A Nice Little Wine
Alf feels that he and Else are entitled to home help. However, it seems that the perfect person for the job is a very flamboyant gay black man. S1 E3
Larry is staying with Robin having been thrown out of his flat, but Larry moves in when the Ropers decide to convert the attic into another flat. S3 E1: Cuckoo in the Nest
The last night of the holiday finds both couples packing, but David and Linda decide to slip away and meet for one last time. S2 E7: Adios

When an author friend of Arthur learns her ex-husband has sold her house without permission, she hires Terry to help with ideas and Arthur to help get the money back. S5 E4: The Second Time Around
Lord Southwold has died as Lady Southwold and a companion visit in London, but a valuable brooch goes missing and the staff come under suspicion. S2 E9: An Object of Value
Mrs Boswell is saying prayers at the table, as usual. Freddie's heart trouble is a worry for the entire Boswell family. S5 E6
The bills are piling up but there's no money to pay for them; as the creditors and bailiffs arrive in the yard, Albert and Harold decide to take drastic action. S4 E4: The Siege of Steptoe Street
The workers of Hatley railway station live a gentle and predictable life, but that is rudely interrupted by some alarming news and a new station master. S1 E1: All Change

When Phoebe's husband Donald returns after escaping from a prisoner of war camp in Italy, Gary feels that he must give Phoebe up for good. S3 E8: There's Something About A Soldier
The strike continues as Bill works out that a coal seam runs under his house, and Tom pursues two kids who have stolen from him but takes pity on them when he discovers why. S1 E5: Coal Comfort

Sitcom. Penny is aching to tell Vince that she doesn't want to see him again, but he hasn't contacted her for over three weeks. S1 E5: I Don't Want to See You Again
There is a formal dinner dance at the firm where Jo and Chrissy work, but Chrissy's dance partner has injured himself and so Robin becomes her partner instead. S3 E3: I Won't Dance, Don't Ask Me
Classic sitcom about a one-man disaster area. Frank attends an RAF reunion and the hidden story of his short but eventful spell in the services unfolds in flashback. S2 E2: The RAF Reunion
Tracey is out of prison, and Sharon gets a job as a school dinner supervisor and recruits Garth as her cook and Tracey as her washer-up. S11 E6: The Chief, The Cook, His Mum and Her Lodger

Jacqueline, assisted by Kenneth and the visiting Troy, scatters her late husband's ashes over Benidorm. S8 E1
